novel_features.name: Resumable album bulk download
novel_features.description: Bulk-download every asset in a curated NASA album with byte-range resume — re-runs pick up exactly where the last failed transfer left off.
novel_features.rationale: NASA's web UI offers no bulk-download; the Python nasa-images-cli restarts every failed file from byte zero. We use the /album/{name} + /asset/{id} pipeline plus a local downloads ledger to support real byte-range resume.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you need 200 NASA images for a deck or archive, this is the one command that finishes — flaky wifi, sleep-resume, mid-run cancellation all become non-events.

novel_features.name: Caption text fetch
novel_features.description: Returns the captions file CONTENT (.srt or .vtt), not just the URL — with an optional `--format text` mode that strips cue numbers and timecodes for readable transcripts.
novel_features.rationale: Every existing NIVL wrapper returns only the captions location URL. Editors and agents end up curl-ing the URL themselves. We follow the indirection and surface the text.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents pulling NASA video captions for analysis, search, or quote-finding skip an extra HTTP call and a parse step.
novel_features.group: API indirections we follow for you

novel_features.name: Metadata sidecar with leak-field filter
novel_features.description: Follows the /metadata/{id} indirection, fetches the metadata.json sidecar, flattens AVAIL:* and EXIF fields, and drops internal-path leak fields (SourceFile, File:Directory, AVAIL:Owner).
novel_features.rationale: NASA's metadata indirection leaks the ingest server's tempfile path and the curator's login name. Every wrapper either returns the URL or pipes the noisy sidecar through. We clean it up.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Journalists and educators get the bylined photo credit (photographer + center + date) without scrubbing noise fields by hand.
novel_features.group: API indirections we follow for you

novel_features.name: Deterministic best-variant picker
novel_features.description: Parses an asset's rendition manifest, classifies each file by variant (orig/large/medium/small/thumb), applies a caller preference order with optional byte ceiling, and prints exactly one URL.
novel_features.rationale: Agents currently compose /search + /asset/{id} + parse-prose-filenames to download a NASA image at a specific resolution. We collapse all of that into one deterministic command.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Claude agents in MCP can ask for 'the best version of nasa_id X under 5 MB' and get exactly one URL — no token spend on parsing filenames.

novel_features.name: Local chronological FTS search (recent)
novel_features.description: FTS5 search over the synced local mirror (title, description, description_508, keywords, album), with chronological sort the upstream API doesn't expose.
novel_features.rationale: NASA's upstream search is keyword-only with no chronological sort (open issue nasa/api-docs#187, no maintainer response). Without a local mirror, finding the most recent assets on a topic is impossible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When breaking news hits, you can pull the most recent NASA images on a topic in milliseconds — without paging through decades of archival results.

novel_features.name: NASA center profile
novel_features.description: Local aggregation over the synced cache: counts by media_type, year-bucket histogram, top keywords, and top photographers for one of the 11 NASA centers.
novel_features.rationale: Each NASA center (JPL = Mars/outer planets, JSC = human spaceflight, KSC = launches, GSFC = Earth and Webb, etc.) has a distinct content profile. The API doesn't expose this aggregate — only the local mirror can.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Journalists answer 'which center publishes this kind of image' before they search — saving search time on stories where a center is the obvious source.

novel_features.name: Unused-in-album anti-join
novel_features.description: Lists nasa_ids in a curated album that haven't been downloaded yet locally — anti-join against the downloads ledger.
novel_features.rationale: Educators and journalists building decks track 'used vs not used' in side notes today. The download command writes a ledger; this command queries it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Plan next week's slides without picking the same image twice — without keeping a manual list of nasa_ids you've already used.

novel_features.name: Topic timeline histogram
novel_features.description: Local GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', date_created) over FTS-matched rows; prints month-bucket counts showing when a topic got coverage.
novel_features.rationale: The upstream API has no chronological surface. Asking 'when did Perseverance go quiet, then publish again?' is unanswerable without a local mirror.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Spot publishing patterns — quiet months, new-release bursts — that surface story angles a keyword search hides.

novel_features.name: Citation string generator
novel_features.description: Generates a ready-to-paste citation string (APA / MLA / Chicago) from cached metadata (photographer, date, center, nasa_id, URL).
novel_features.rationale: Journalists and educators hand-assemble citations from metadata.json today. A pure string template over a single metadata row covers every published style.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Drop a NASA image in a piece and paste the citation underneath — no formatting by hand.

narrative.headline: Every NASA Image and Video Library endpoint, plus a local SQLite mirror, FTS search, resumable bulk download, caption text extraction, and an agent-native MCP surface no other tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: The official NASA Image and Video Library exposes five endpoints but no Go CLI covers all of them, and none of the existing wrappers go past returning JSON. nasa-images-pp-cli adds a local SQLite mirror with FTS5 search, byte-range resumable album downloads, caption text extraction (not just URLs), deterministic best-variant picking for agents, and a typed MCP server — every command works offline once synced, and every command pipes cleanly to jq.

quickstart.comment: search the live API with all the filters NASA exposes
get exactly one URL for the best variant under a size cap — no manifest parsing
bulk-download a curated album with byte-range resume; re-runs pick up where they left off
populate the local SQLite mirror for offline FTS search via mirror search and timeline queries
chronologically-sorted FTS search NASA's upstream API doesn't offer
actual caption text, not the .srt URL every other wrapper stops at

troubleshoots.fix: Check spelling and try a broader --q; the upstream API uses keyword matching only. For breaking-news content, run `mirror search --q <topic>` first then use `recent --sort date-desc`.
Album names are exactly case-sensitive — `Apollo-at-50` works, `apollo-at-50` does not. Discover album names from search results' `data[].album` field.

troubleshoots.symptom: 400 from captions fetch with mediatype=image error
troubleshoots.fix: Captions only exist for video assets. Run `assets get <nasa_id>` first to confirm media_type=video.

troubleshoots.symptom: metadata fetch returns AVAIL:Owner or SourceFile fields
troubleshoots.fix: Upgrade — current version filters these leak fields by default. The upstream sidecar still leaks them, but our metadata fetch strips them.
Re-run the same `download album` command with `--resume`. The local downloads ledger tracks per-file progress, and in-flight files resume from the last completed byte.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent or workflow needs deterministic access to NASA's public-domain media archive — image search, asset rendition picking, caption extraction, bulk album archiving — without parsing prose. The local SQLite mirror makes repeat queries free and unlocks chronological/topic-timeline analyses the upstream API doesn't support. The MCP surface gives Claude agents typed tools for every endpoint plus the novel composed commands (assets best, captions fetch text, citation, etc.).

recipes.title: Find a recent Mars image and pick the best variant
recipes.explanation: Lists recent Mars rover images; pick a nasa_id and pass it to `assets best` for the best variant URL.

recipes.title: Archive a full curated album
recipes.explanation: Walks every page of the album, fetches the asset manifest per item, then byte-range-resumes each variant download.

recipes.title: Pull a video transcript
recipes.command: nasa-images-pp-cli captions fetch jsc2022m000123 --format text > webb-deployment.txt
recipes.explanation: Caption indirection is two GETs; --format text strips SRT cue numbers for clean transcripts.

recipes.title: Build an offline FTS search index
recipes.command: nasa-images-pp-cli mirror search --q "apollo" --year-start 1968 --year-end 1975 && nasa-images-pp-cli recent --q "bootprint" --sort date-desc --json --select 'nasa_id,title,date_created'
recipes.explanation: sync populates the SQLite mirror; search local uses FTS5 and adds chronological sort NASA's upstream search doesn't expose.

recipes.title: Generate an APA citation
recipes.explanation: Pulls the metadata sidecar (cached or live) and formats a ready-to-paste citation string.
